projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

needs_attention.title: Contact installers for quotes

insights.summary: The sustained presence of 16-19 mobile devices combined with zero camera motion over four hours on a Saturday night strongly suggests a large group of guests is gathered in non-camera zones or low-light areas, which is atypical for a household with active children and dogs. This warrants a quick check to ensure cameras are functioning and guests are safely settled.

The sustained high device count (16-19) combined with zero camera motion over four hours strongly suggests either a house full of guests or a camera system blind spot, as normal evening routines would typically trigger at least one motion sensor.

The sustained high device count (18-20) paired with zero camera activity strongly suggests guests or sleepovers are settled indoors, which explains the lack of motion triggers and aligns with a typical Saturday evening gathering rather than a system fault.

Mobile device count surged from 13 to 19 over the last hour while cameras remain completely inactive, indicating guests have likely arrived and are settling in off-camera areas or bedrooms. This aligns with a typical Saturday evening gathering, but confirming camera functionality and guest status is recommended.

The consistent presence of 12-13 devices alongside zero camera motion throughout the Saturday afternoon strongly indicates the household is gathered indoors for a stationary activity like a movie or gaming session. This confirms everyone is safely home and explains the lack of motion triggers, but warrants a quick check if the activity extends unusually late or impacts energy usage.

The sustained 12-13 device count combined with zero camera motion throughout the Saturday afternoon strongly indicates the house is unoccupied, with personal electronics left running. This cross-signal pattern confirms a typical weekend outing but suggests automating power management or sending a safety check when the home remains empty for extended periods.

The sustained presence of 12-16 connected devices alongside zero camera motion throughout a Saturday afternoon strongly suggests the household is gathered in a camera-blind zone (e.g., basement or garage) for a stationary activity, or reflects a surge of stationary IoT devices. This highlights a potential blind-spot coverage gap worth verifying.

The sustained presence of 12-17 devices combined with zero camera motion during a Saturday midday strongly indicates a planned guest gathering in a camera-blind zone rather than a transient event. This confirms a social state shift that explains the high device count and contextualizes the quiet environment.

The presence count of 12-17 devices significantly exceeds the household's typical 6-8, and combined with zero camera motion over four hours, strongly indicates the presence sensor is detecting external Wi-Fi signals (e.g., neighbors or passersby) rather than actual occupancy.

The sustained spike of 13-17 active mobile devices at Saturday noon, combined with zero camera motion events, strongly suggests an indoor social gathering (likely guests or the kids hosting friends) rather than a network anomaly. This explains the high device count without triggering motion alerts, but worth a quick verification to ensure it's expected.
